description: Yooti Metrics Dashboard — Reference Implementation. Built using the Yooti pipeline. Demonstrates AWS Lambda + DynamoDB + React + LangGraph agent on a real product.

sprints.goal: Ship a working metrics dashboard with real data flowing from .agent/ files through Lambda + DynamoDB to a React UI

stories.title: As a metrics consumer I want a DynamoDB repository layer so that all dashboard data is stored and retrieved without Scan operations

acceptance_criteria.given: a valid sprint payload is written to DynamoDB
acceptance_criteria.when: the same payload is written again with identical sprint_num
acceptance_criteria.then: the write succeeds idempotently — no error, no duplicate
all KPI fields are returned using a single Query — no Scan

acceptance_criteria.given: five story records exist for sprint 10
acceptance_criteria.then: all five records returned using SK begins_with — one Query call
item returned using GetItem — not Query, not Scan
ClientError caught and re-raised as RepositoryError — no internal detail exposed

definition_of_done: All AC have passing unit tests using @mock_aws — no real DynamoDB calls
Single table design confirmed — PK/SK patterns match architecture document
No Scan operations anywhere in repository layer
Conditional writes used on all put_item calls
TABLE_NAME from os.environ — never hardcoded
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Security scan: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL
stories.architect_note: PK=PROJECT#<id>, SK patterns defined in ARCH-DASHBOARD.md Section 3. Enforce single table. No Scan anywhere.
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — DynamoDB table patterns + repository class | T002 — All read methods with access pattern tests | T003 — Write methods with idempotency tests

stories.title: As aggregate-metrics.py I want POST /metrics/ingest so that pipeline data is written to DynamoDB and the current snapshot is updated atomically

acceptance_criteria.given: a valid ingest payload with project_id, sprint_num, and stories array
acceptance_criteria.then: all entities written to DynamoDB and 201 returned with sprint_num

acceptance_criteria.given: the same sprint payload is submitted a second time
acceptance_criteria.then: all items overwritten and 200 returned — not 409
400 returned with error INVALID_PROJECT_ID — no stack trace in response
500 returned with error INTERNAL_ERROR — no internal detail exposed

acceptance_criteria.given: a valid ingest payload is submitted
acceptance_criteria.then: CURRENT#SNAPSHOT updated with new KPIs and insight alerts computed

definition_of_done: All AC passing with @mock_aws — no real DynamoDB calls
Handler returns correct statusCode for every AC
CURRENT#SNAPSHOT updated on every successful ingest
Insight alert types: BOTTLENECK, HUMAN_VS_AGENT, CONSTITUTION_GAP, QUALITY_DROP, REGRESSION_SPIKE, ESCALATION_SPIKE, DORA_GAP
Idempotency verified — same payload twice returns 200 not 409
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Security scan: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — Input validator | T002 — ingest_metrics Lambda handler | T003 — Insight alert engine | T004 — Unit tests all AC

stories.title: As the dashboard I want GET /metrics/current so that the page loads with the latest sprint KPIs and alerts in a single API call

acceptance_criteria.given: a current snapshot exists for project_id
acceptance_criteria.then: 200 returned with kpis, insights, sprint_num, last_ingested_at
404 returned with error SPRINT_NOT_FOUND
400 returned with error INVALID_PROJECT_ID
data retrieved using single GetItem — no Query, no Scan

definition_of_done: All AC passing with @mock_aws
Single GetItem confirmed — not Query or Scan
CORS headers in every response
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Security scan: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — get_current_metrics Lambda | T002 — Unit tests all AC

stories.title: As the sprint selector I want GET /metrics/sprints and GET /metrics/sprint/{id} so that users can browse all historical sprint data

acceptance_criteria.given: five sprints exist for a project
acceptance_criteria.then: array of five summaries returned in descending sprint_num order

acceptance_criteria.given: sprint 10 has summary and eight stories
acceptance_criteria.then: response includes summary KPIs, phase_avg_hrs, gate_rejection_rates, escalation_breakdown, stories array
404 with error SPRINT_NOT_FOUND

acceptance_criteria.given: sprint_num is not a positive integer
acceptance_criteria.then: 400 with error INVALID_SPRINT_NUM

definition_of_done: All AC passing with @mock_aws
No Scan — Query with SK begins_with only
Results sorted descending by sprint_num
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Security scan: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — get_sprint_list Lambda | T002 — get_sprint_detail Lambda | T003 — Unit tests all AC

stories.title: As the trends and story tabs I want GET /metrics/trends and GET /metrics/story/{id} so that users see metric history and per-story detail

acceptance_criteria.given: five trend points exist for cycle_time_avg
acceptance_criteria.then: array of five objects with sprint_num, value, delta_from_prev, direction returned
all metric series returned grouped by metric_name
response includes evidence fields, phase_durations, gate_timestamps with reviewers, escalation_log
404 with error STORY_NOT_FOUND

definition_of_done: All AC passing with @mock_aws
Trend data sorted ascending by sprint_num for charting
Story detail includes gate_timestamps, phase_durations, escalation_log
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Security scan: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — get_trends Lambda | T002 — get_story_detail Lambda | T003 — Unit tests all AC

stories.title: As the system I want a SAM template wiring all Lambdas to API Gateway so that the full backend deploys with one command locally and to AWS

acceptance_criteria.then: template is valid with zero errors

acceptance_criteria.when: sam local start-api --env-vars env.local.json is run
acceptance_criteria.then: all six endpoints respond correctly to curl test requests

acceptance_criteria.given: Vite dev server is running with proxy config
acceptance_criteria.when: fetch to /api/metrics/current is made from browser
acceptance_criteria.then: request proxies to LocalStack and returns data — no CORS error

definition_of_done: sam validate passes with zero errors
All six endpoints wired in template.yaml
DynamoDB table resource with single table schema
TABLE_NAME passed as env var to all Lambdas
CORS configured for Vite dev origin
env.local.json generated for LocalStack
samconfig.toml generated for staging and production
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — template.yaml all Lambdas + API Gateway + DynamoDB | T002 — samconfig.toml + env.local.json + Vite proxy config

stories.title: As yooti sprint:report I want aggregate-metrics.py to read .agent/ files and POST to ingest so that the dashboard updates automatically after every sprint report

acceptance_criteria.given: a Yooti project with gate files and evidence packages for sprint 10
acceptance_criteria.then: valid ingest payload built and POST /metrics/ingest returns 201

acceptance_criteria.given: a gate file is missing for one story
acceptance_criteria.then: story included with status INCOMPLETE — no error or skip

acceptance_criteria.given: aggregate-metrics.py run twice for same sprint
acceptance_criteria.then: ingest returns 200 — idempotent re-ingest succeeds
clear error message printed and exit code 1 — no silent failure
aggregate-metrics.py called automatically and dashboard update message printed

definition_of_done: aggregate-metrics.py reads all gate files, evidence, escalations, corrections, logs
Computes all KPI fields in ingest payload schema
Called automatically by yooti sprint:report
YOOTI_DASHBOARD_API_URL from os.environ — not hardcoded
No real API call in unit tests — requests.post mocked
Coverage on new code >= 90%
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — File readers for each .agent/ folder type | T002 — Metric computation | T003 — HTTP ingest + sprint:report integration | T004 — Unit tests all AC

stories.title: As a dashboard user I want React fetch hooks for all API endpoints so that every tab loads real data with loading and error states

acceptance_criteria.then: GET /metrics/current called and KPI cards populate within 2 seconds — loading skeleton shown while fetching
inline error state shown with retry button — page does not crash
GET /metrics/sprint/9 called and all tab data updates — no full page reload
all requests proxy to LocalStack via Vite proxy — no direct localhost:4566 URLs in component code

definition_of_done: Hooks: useCurrentMetrics, useSprintList, useSprintDetail, useTrends, useStoryDetail
Loading skeleton during fetch
Error state with retry for every hook
Sprint selector triggers correct API call
VITE_API_BASE_URL from .env — not hardcoded
All hooks tested with MSW — no real API calls in tests
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Accessibility: 0 axe-core violations
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — API client + all five hooks | T002 — Sprint selector component | T003 — MSW handlers and hook tests

stories.title: As an engineering manager I want a six-tab React dashboard rendering real pipeline metrics so that I can make data-driven decisions about team productivity, quality, and agent efficiency

acceptance_criteria.given: real metrics data available from API
acceptance_criteria.when: each of the six tabs is viewed
acceptance_criteria.then: all KPI cards, charts, and tables render with real data — no hardcoded values

acceptance_criteria.given: an insight alert exists in current snapshot
acceptance_criteria.then: insight strip renders with correct message and severity colour
all content readable — no horizontal scroll, no overlapping elements

acceptance_criteria.given: a story row is clicked in Story drill-down tab
acceptance_criteria.then: GET /metrics/story/{id} called and story detail panel opens with gate timeline, phase durations, escalation log
zero violations

definition_of_done: Six tabs: Productivity, Quality, Agent efficiency, Value, DORA, Story drill-down
All charts use Chart.js with real API data
KPI trend arrows colour-coded: down-good green, up-bad red, by metric direction
Insight alerts with severity styling
Story drill-down with click-through to story detail panel
Responsive at 320px, 768px, 1280px
Accessibility: 0 axe-core violations
Coverage on new code >= 90%
Lighthouse performance >= 80
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — Shared components: KPI card, chart wrapper, insight strip, trend badge | T002 — Productivity and DORA tabs | T003 — Quality and Agent efficiency tabs | T004 — Value tab + Story drill-down with detail panel

sprints.goal: Enrich dashboard metrics with GitHub PR data so that Pickup Time, Review Time, PR Size, and Review Depth appear alongside Yooti-native metrics

stories.title: As the GitHub enrichment agent I want a typed LangGraph state definition so that state is immutable, resumable from any node, and every node is independently testable

acceptance_criteria.given: a list of story dicts and a sprint number
acceptance_criteria.then: all required fields present with correct types — no mutable defaults
current_idx is 5 — graph can resume from this point if interrupted

acceptance_criteria.given: a node returns an error dict
acceptance_criteria.then: error appended to errors list — previous enriched results not lost

definition_of_done: GitHubMetricsState defined as TypedDict — no mutable defaults
All fields typed: story list, enriched list, error list, rate_limit_reset
EnrichedStory TypedDict with all PR metric fields
EnrichmentError TypedDict with error_type, message, timestamp
State unit tests — pure dict operations, no LLM calls, no graph execution
Coverage on new code >= 90%
stories.architect_note: CRITICAL: This agent makes ZERO LLM calls. LangGraph used for state management and routing only. No prompts. No model costs per run. Confirm in plan annotations.
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — state.py with all TypedDict definitions | T002 — Unit tests for state initialisation and field validation

stories.title: As the agent graph I want GitHub fetch nodes so that each node does one thing, returns state update dicts, and never raises uncaught exceptions

acceptance_criteria.given: a story ID with a merged PR on branch feature/GHAGENT-002
acceptance_criteria.then: state updated with pr_number and pr_url — no exception raised
node returns state update with error_type RATE_LIMIT and reset timestamp — does not raise

acceptance_criteria.given: no PR exists for the story branch
acceptance_criteria.then: state updated with pr_status NO_PR_FOUND — no error

acceptance_criteria.given: PR has 3 reviews and 7 inline comments
acceptance_criteria.then: pickup_time_hrs, review_time_hrs, pr_size_lines, review_comment_count correct in state

definition_of_done: Five nodes: load_stories, find_pr, fetch_pr_details, fetch_reviews_and_comments, compute_metrics
Every node returns dict — never mutates state in place
Every node catches all exceptions — returns error dict, never raises
GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPO from os.environ — never hardcoded
All nodes tested with mocked requests.get — no real GitHub API calls
No LLM calls in any node
Coverage on new code >= 90%
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — nodes/find_pr.py + nodes/fetch_pr_details.py | T002 — nodes/fetch_reviews.py + nodes/compute_metrics.py + nodes/load_stories.py | T003 — Unit tests for all five nodes

stories.title: As the agent graph I want conditional edges and error routing so that rate limits trigger automatic retry, missing PRs are skipped gracefully, and auth errors halt the run cleanly

acceptance_criteria.then: graph routes to handle_error which waits then retries — story not skipped
graph routes to accumulate_and_advance — story marked no_pr, processing continues
graph routes to END — no further GitHub calls, clear error logged

acceptance_criteria.given: 10 stories with story 7 having no PR
acceptance_criteria.then: stories 1-6 and 8-10 enriched, story 7 marked no_pr — graph does not halt
graph routes to post_to_ingest_api — not back to find_pr

definition_of_done: Routing functions: route_after_find_pr, route_after_fetch, route_after_accumulate, route_after_error
handle_error node waits until X-RateLimit-Reset then retries
API_5XX retried up to 3 times then story skipped
Full graph compiled with MemorySaver checkpointer
Integration test: 10 mocked stories mix of found/not-found/rate-limit
Integration test: auth error halts at correct story
No LLM calls in any test
Coverage on new code >= 90%
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — graph.py wiring all nodes and edges | T002 — nodes/accumulate.py + nodes/handle_error.py + nodes/post_to_ingest.py | T003 — Integration tests: full graph all scenarios

stories.title: As yooti sprint:report I want the GitHub agent invoked automatically so that PR enrichment reaches the dashboard without any manual steps

acceptance_criteria.then: GitHub agent runs after aggregate-metrics.py and enriched metrics appear in dashboard
sprint:report completes normally with a warning — no error, no halt

acceptance_criteria.given: agent completes with 2 stories having no PR
acceptance_criteria.then: output shows: '13 stories enriched from GitHub, 2 skipped (no PR found)'
message printed: 'GitHub rate limit — resuming in Xm Ys' — process does not exit

definition_of_done: Agent runner invoked from sprint:report after base aggregation
GITHUB_TOKEN absence handled gracefully — warning not error
Summary line printed: N enriched, N skipped
Rate limit wait message with time printed
No real GitHub or API calls in unit tests — all mocked
Coverage on new code >= 90%
stories.decomposition_hint: T001 — agents/github_metrics/runner.py wrapping graph invocation | T002 — Integration into sprint:report with env check and summary print | T003 — Unit tests all AC with mocked graph
